stimulus.title: Correction Workflow case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 7069 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove JSONL and CSV contracts, accessible HTML, chart alternatives, corrections, and deterministic evidence; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Link findings to evidence, owner, status, decision, suppression expiry, retest, changed version, and stakeholder-readable resolution.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Deleting a report row is a valid correction history.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Link findings to evidence, owner, status, decision, suppression expiry, retest, changed version, and stakeholder-readable resolution. Artifact under review: versioned accessible site-quality report suite.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Correction Workflow, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Correction Workflow record says versioned accessible site-quality report suite looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Correction Workflow.

For separate evidence from confidence, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. This does not satisfy Correction Workflow for versioned accessible site-quality report suite.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Correction Workflow
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Correction Workflow: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review TypeScript team handles case 5450 during an origin-fairness and event-loop capacity rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove JSONL and CSV contracts, accessible HTML, chart alternatives, corrections, and deterministic evidence; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Link findings to evidence, owner, status, decision, suppression expiry, retest, changed version, and stakeholder-readable resolution.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Correction Workflow.
steps.why: Retrieving Correction Workflow through the concrete versioned accessible site-quality report suite case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Correction Workflow, keep Correction Workflow connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Link findings to evidence, owner, status, decision, suppression expiry, retest, changed version, and stakeholder-readable resolution.

checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
